brief.name: Lyon Stahl Investment Real Estate
brief.what_they_do: A multifamily investment brokerage serving Los Angeles and Southern California, specializing in acquisitions, dispositions, and 1031 exchanges for multifamily assets. They operate as a commercial real estate brokerage with 125+ agents across 4 offices.

signals.detail: Over $5.9 billion closed across more than 3,600 transactions
#1 Multifamily Market Share

CoStar Power Broker Top Sales Firm in Los Angeles in 2023, 2024, and 2025; GlobeSt. 2025 Influencer in Multifamily; LA Times Studios 2025 Top Commercial Real Estate Firm; Inc. 5000 fastest-growing private companies

Founded in 2014 by Brett Lyon and Woody Stahl, grown from a two-person office in El Segundo into one of the most active multifamily investment brokerages in Southern California with 125+ agents and 4 offices
Specialize in multifamily acquisitions, dispositions, and 1031 exchanges across Los Angeles, Long Beach, the South Bay, and the Inland Empire

i'm johnny — an independent AI-automations marketer. i build the data+story layer for sales teams: scraping, enrichment, CRM-from-scratch, agent systems. receipts: built Vortex for a marketing agency on a signed $5,040 two-month contract; ~40% LinkedIn reply rates vs 2% cold email. the system replaces the manual research hire.
PERSON SCRAPE DEGRADED: person stage skipped: no person inputs on the run (linkedinUrl/xHandle/name all absent)
Real-estate investor client agreed $4-5k flat + 10-20% commission ('anywhere from 4 5 K would be sick')
Marketing agency signed fixed $5,040 / 2 months / 4 modules + ~$250/mo running costs
'name + linkedin' Google-search hack: ~85% hit rate where scrapers get accounts flagged
LinkedIn outreach ~40% reply rate vs cold email ~2% ('if you're doing like good')
The pricing anchor to beat: refused vendor at '$15,000 and it's like 40 enriched'

brief.brief: Lyon Stahl Investment Real Estate is a dominant multifamily brokerage in Southern California founded in 2014 by Brett Lyon and Woody Stahl, now operating 125+ agents across 4 offices with $5.9B in closed transactions and #1 multifamily market share. The firm specializes in acquisitions, dispositions, and 1031 exchanges for multifamily assets across Los Angeles, Long Beach, South Bay, and Inland Empire, emphasizing collaborative culture and proprietary market intelligence. Recognized as a CoStar Power Broker Top Firm (2023-2025), GlobeSt. Influencer, and Inc. 5000 fastest-growing company, they position themselves as a knowledge-sharing alternative to traditional gatekeeping brokerages.

generations.story:
THE GOAL:
Lyon Stahl wants to be the undisputed first call for every serious multifamily seller and buyer in Southern California — not just the biggest firm, but the most informed one. Their own site frames it as 'knowledge-sharing over gatekeeping' [company_scale, market_position]. With $5.9B closed across 3,600+ transactions and #1 multifamily market share [company_scale], the goal isn't survival — it's compounding dominance: more agents producing, more deals sourced before they hit the open market, more 1031 exchange clients who stay in the Lyon Stahl ecosystem for the next trade [geographic_coverage].

THE OBSTACLE:
125+ agents across 4 offices [founding_and_growth] means 125+ individual prospecting motions — each one manually researching owners, guessing at motivation, cold-emailing into the void. The firm's edge is supposed to be proprietary market intelligence [key_features], but at that agent count, 'intelligence' is only as good as the slowest researcher on the team. The real obstacle: the gap between what the firm knows at the top (deal flow, owner signals, motivation reads) and what each agent actually has in their hands when they pick up the phone. That gap is where deals leak to competitors.

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
The standard playbook — pull a list, cold-call, blast email — is documented to fail at scale. Cold email sits at roughly 2% reply rate [seller_proof]. Manual enrichment is inconsistently executed across a 125-person org, and off-the-shelf scrapers get accounts flagged [seller_proof]. A firm that's won CoStar Power Broker three consecutive years (2023–2025) [awards_recognition] and made Inc. 5000 [awards_recognition] cannot afford to have its agents working from data that's 30% wrong or six months stale — because a wrong owner name or a missed motivation signal is exactly how a relationship brokerage loses the relationship before it starts.

THE BETTER PATH:
Johnny builds the data-and-story layer that sits underneath every agent's outreach — not a one-time list pull, but a durable, rerunnable system: scraping, enrichment, motivation reads, CRM-ready records [seller_positioning]. The 'name + LinkedIn' Google-search hack hits ~85% where scrapers get flagged [seller_proof]. LinkedIn outreach built on that enriched layer runs ~40% reply rates versus the 2% cold-email baseline the firm is likely tolerating now [seller_proof]. The pricing model is flat base plus commission — a real-estate investor client already agreed $4–5K flat plus 10–20% commission on deals sourced [seller_proof] — structured so Lyon Stahl's ops budget is predictable and the incentive is aligned to closed transactions, not vanity metrics. The system replaces the manual research hire that every growing brokerage eventually over-pays for [seller_positioning].

Opening angle, in Johnny's voice: 'You've got 125 agents and the #1 market share — the question is whether each of them is walking into calls with the same quality of owner intelligence you have at the top, or whether that gap is where your deals are quietly going to someone else.'

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lyon Stahl's agents stop prospecting blind. Each one carries enriched, motivation-read owner profiles — the kind of context that turns a cold call into a conversation where the owner feels understood before the agent has said ten words. The firm's proprietary intelligence reputation, already recognized by GlobeSt. and CoStar [awards_recognition], becomes a real operational asset at the agent level, not just a marketing claim. Deal sourcing gets faster, 1031 exchange pipelines get warmer earlier, and the knowledge-sharing culture they were founded on [founding_and_growth] finally has a data infrastructure to match the ambition. Lyon Stahl is in a powerful position because they already have the brand — this gives them the engine to make it compound.

generations.story:
THE GOAL:
Lyon Stahl wants to be the dominant multifamily brokerage in Southern California — not just by volume, but by intelligence. Their site positions them as the knowledge-sharing alternative to gatekeeping brokerages: 'collaborative culture,' 'proprietary market intelligence,' deal sourcing that beats the market. The goal is agent productivity at scale — 125+ agents [company_scale], four offices [founding_and_growth], and a pipeline that keeps every one of them closing.

THE OBSTACLE:
At that agent count, the data problem compounds fast. Each agent needs enriched, story-ready lead records — owner identity, life-stage signals, contact paths — before a single outreach goes out. The manual research burden per agent, multiplied across a firm this size, is enormous. And the signals that actually move sellers (absentee fatigue, inheritance pressure, equity position) live in county records and LinkedIn profiles that scrapers routinely miss or get flagged pulling [seller_proof].

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
The standard vendor path — bulk list pulls, generic enrichment — doesn't hold up. A competitor pitch Johnny already reviewed was '$15,000 and it's like 40 enriched' [seller_proof]: expensive, thin, and not rerunnable. Cold email as the delivery layer compounds the problem — industry reply rates sit around 2% [seller_proof], meaning even a clean list gets buried. For a brokerage ranked CoStar Power Broker Top Sales Firm three consecutive years [awards_recognition] and named among the fastest-growing private companies [awards_recognition], a leaky data layer is the ceiling on how far that growth goes.

THE BETTER PATH:
Johnny builds the data-and-story layer that sits underneath the agents' outreach — scraping, enrichment, CRM-from-scratch — as a durable system, not automation slop that needs rebuilding every two months [seller_positioning]. The 'name + LinkedIn' Google-search hack hits roughly 85% of targets where scrapers get accounts flagged [seller_proof]. LinkedIn outreach on enriched, story-ready records runs around 40% reply rate versus the 2% cold email baseline [seller_proof]. The model is a flat base plus commission — a marketing agency signed at a fixed rate for two months covering four modules, plus low monthly running costs [seller_proof]; a real-estate investor client landed in the $4–5k flat plus 10–20% commission range [seller_proof]. The system replaces the manual research hire and scales across agent count without rebuilding.
Opening angle, in Johnny's voice: 'You've got 125 agents working Southern California multifamily — the constraint isn't hustle, it's that the data layer underneath their outreach isn't built to match the story each seller is actually living right now.'

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lyon Stahl's agents go into every call with a grounded working theory on the seller — life-stage read, contact path, the right angle — not a name and a phone number. Reply rates move from noise-floor cold email into LinkedIn territory [seller_proof]. The CRM becomes a rerunnable asset the firm owns, not a vendor dependency. And the intelligence edge that Lyon Stahl already markets as their differentiator [company_scale, market_position] gets operationalized at the agent level — which is where the next phase of growth either compounds or stalls.

generations.story:
THE GOAL:
Lyon Stahl wants to be the dominant multifamily brokerage in Southern California — not just by transaction volume, but by being the firm that knows more, moves faster, and recruits better than anyone else in the market. Their own site language is about 'market intelligence,' 'deal sourcing,' and a 'collaborative culture' that attracts agents away from gatekeeping shops. Growth is the operating thesis: from a two-person El Segundo office in 2014 to 125+ agents across 4 offices today [founding_and_growth]. The goal isn't stability — it's compounding scale.

THE OBSTACLE:
At 125+ agents across 4 offices [company_scale], the bottleneck shifts from hustle to infrastructure. Each agent is prospecting multifamily owners across Los Angeles, Long Beach, South Bay, and Inland Empire [geographic_coverage] — that's a massive, overlapping, constantly-changing lead universe. The firm's edge is supposed to be proprietary market intelligence [key_features], but if 125 agents are each doing manual owner research — pulling county records, Googling names, cold-emailing into the void — the intelligence layer is only as good as whoever did the research last week. The data degrades faster than anyone can keep up with it.

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
Cold email at scale gets ~2% reply rates [seller_proof]. Scrapers get accounts flagged [seller_proof]. Manual research doesn't replicate across a 125-agent team — it lives in someone's head or a spreadsheet that goes stale. Vendors in this space charge prices like $15,000 for 40 enriched records [seller_proof] — that math doesn't work when you're running thousands of owner lookups across four markets. And any automation built on fragile scraper stacks needs to be rebuilt every few months, meaning the 'system' never compounds. For a firm whose entire positioning rests on knowing the market better than the competition [market_position], a leaky, inconsistent data layer is a direct threat to the brand promise.

THE BETTER PATH:
Johnny builds the data and story layer that a 125-agent multifamily shop actually needs: scraping, enrichment, and a durable CRM-style system that reruns without breaking [seller_positioning]. The 'name + LinkedIn' Google-search method hits ~85% accuracy where scrapers get flagged [seller_proof] — meaning Lyon Stahl's agents reach real decision-makers instead of dead ends. LinkedIn outreach built on enriched, story-grounded records pulls ~40% reply rates versus the ~2% cold email baseline [seller_proof]. The pricing structure is flat base plus commission — a real-estate investor client already validated the $4–5k flat + 10–20% commission model [seller_proof], which maps directly to how Lyon Stahl already thinks about deal economics. The system is built once, runs continuously, and replaces the manual research hire — it doesn't need to be rebuilt every two months [seller_positioning].
Opening angle, in Johnny's voice: 'You've built the #1 multifamily market share position in LA [market_position] — the data layer underneath your agents' prospecting should be compounding the same way your transaction volume is.'

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lyon Stahl's agents stop doing manual owner research and start doing what they're actually paid to do — closing multifamily deals. The firm gets a rerunnable enrichment system across all four markets [geographic_coverage] that feeds consistent, high-accuracy owner data into every agent's outreach. Reply rates move from noise toward signal. The market intelligence positioning they already claim becomes operationally real — not a marketing line, but a system. And as they continue recruiting agents [agent_recruitment], each new hire plugs into infrastructure that's already working, instead of reinventing the research wheel from scratch. The compounding they've built on the transaction side finally has a data side to match it.

brief.brief: Lyon Stahl Investment Real Estate is a dominant multifamily brokerage in Southern California founded in 2014 by Brett Lyon and Woody Stahl, now operating 125+ agents across 4 offices with $5.9B in closed transactions and #1 multifamily market share. The firm specializes in acquisitions, dispositions, and 1031 exchanges for multifamily assets across Los Angeles, Long Beach, South Bay, and Inland Empire, emphasizing collaborative culture and proprietary market intelligence. Recognized as a CoStar Power Broker Top Firm (2023-2025), GlobeSt. Influencer, and Inc. 5000 fastest-growing company, they position themselves as a knowledge-sharing alternative to traditional gatekeeping brokerages.

event.story:
THE GOAL:
Lyon Stahl wants to be the undisputed first call for every serious multifamily seller and buyer in Southern California — not just the biggest firm, but the most informed one. Their own site frames it as 'knowledge-sharing over gatekeeping' [company_scale, market_position]. With $5.9B closed across 3,600+ transactions and #1 multifamily market share [company_scale], the goal isn't survival — it's compounding dominance: more agents producing, more deals sourced before they hit the open market, more 1031 exchange clients who stay in the Lyon Stahl ecosystem for the next trade [geographic_coverage].

THE OBSTACLE:
125+ agents across 4 offices [founding_and_growth] means 125+ individual prospecting motions — each one manually researching owners, guessing at motivation, cold-emailing into the void. The firm's edge is supposed to be proprietary market intelligence [key_features], but at that agent count, 'intelligence' is only as good as the slowest researcher on the team. The real obstacle: the gap between what the firm knows at the top (deal flow, owner signals, motivation reads) and what each agent actually has in their hands when they pick up the phone. That gap is where deals leak to competitors.

THE OLD WAY FAILS:
The standard playbook — pull a list, cold-call, blast email — is documented to fail at scale. Cold email sits at roughly 2% reply rate [seller_proof]. Manual enrichment is inconsistently executed across a 125-person org, and off-the-shelf scrapers get accounts flagged [seller_proof]. A firm that's won CoStar Power Broker three consecutive years (2023–2025) [awards_recognition] and made Inc. 5000 [awards_recognition] cannot afford to have its agents working from data that's 30% wrong or six months stale — because a wrong owner name or a missed motivation signal is exactly how a relationship brokerage loses the relationship before it starts.

THE BETTER PATH:
Johnny builds the data-and-story layer that sits underneath every agent's outreach — not a one-time list pull, but a durable, rerunnable system: scraping, enrichment, motivation reads, CRM-ready records [seller_positioning]. The 'name + LinkedIn' Google-search hack hits ~85% where scrapers get flagged [seller_proof]. LinkedIn outreach built on that enriched layer runs ~40% reply rates versus the 2% cold-email baseline the firm is likely tolerating now [seller_proof]. The pricing model is flat base plus commission — a real-estate investor client already agreed $4–5K flat plus 10–20% commission on deals sourced [seller_proof] — structured so Lyon Stahl's ops budget is predictable and the incentive is aligned to closed transactions, not vanity metrics. The system replaces the manual research hire that every growing brokerage eventually over-pays for [seller_positioning].

Opening angle, in Johnny's voice: 'You've got 125 agents and the #1 market share — the question is whether each of them is walking into calls with the same quality of owner intelligence you have at the top, or whether that gap is where your deals are quietly going to someone else.'

THE BETTER OUTCOME:
Lyon Stahl's agents stop prospecting blind. Each one carries enriched, motivation-read owner profiles — the kind of context that turns a cold call into a conversation where the owner feels understood before the agent has said ten words. The firm's proprietary intelligence reputation, already recognized by GlobeSt. and CoStar [awards_recognition], becomes a real operational asset at the agent level, not just a marketing claim. Deal sourcing gets faster, 1031 exchange pipelines get warmer earlier, and the knowledge-sharing culture they were founded on [founding_and_growth] finally has a data infrastructure to match the ambition. Lyon Stahl is in a powerful position because they already have the brand — this gives them the engine to make it compound.
